transport.c(3) Library Functions Manual transport.c(3)

NAME

transport.c - WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64
Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). #define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512
Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). #define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING
#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Functions


acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char *name, const char *url, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket client transport. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create (const char *name, struct lws *wsi, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection.

Detailed Description

WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

Implements the acip_transport_t interface for WebSocket connections. Enables browser clients to connect via WebSocket protocol.

ARCHITECTURE:

  • Uses libwebsockets for WebSocket protocol handling
  • Async libwebsockets callbacks bridge to sync recv() via ringbuffer
  • Thread-safe receive queue handles async message arrival
  • Same pattern as WebRTC transport for consistency

MESSAGE FLOW:

1.
send(): Synchronous write via lws_write()
2.
LWS callback: Async write to receive ringbuffer
3.
recv(): Blocking read from receive ringbuffer

MEMORY OWNERSHIP:

  • Transport OWNS wsi (WebSocket instance)
  • Receive queue owns buffered message data
  • recv() allocates message buffer, caller must free

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file websocket/transport.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64

Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). Set to 64 for balance: allows buffering across one large frame's fragments while keeping latency reasonable. With ~70 fragments per 291KB frame, we need at least 64 slots to avoid dropping the frame while it's fragmenting.

Definition at line 59 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512

Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). Set to 64 to match incoming queue and prevent "BINARY after FIN" errors. Large video frames fragment into ~70 chunks; queue must be large enough to buffer one complete message worth of fragments.

Definition at line 68 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING

Definition at line 71 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Definition at line 72 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

Function Documentation

acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char * name, const char * url, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)

Create WebSocket client transport.

Parameters

url WebSocket URL (e.g., "ws://localhost:27225")
crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)

Returns

Transport instance or NULL on failure

Definition at line 1378 of file websocket/transport.c.

1379                                                                                        {
1380   if (!name) {
1381     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1382     return NULL;
1383   }
1384 
1385   if (!url) {
1386     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "url is required");
1387     return NULL;
1388   }
1389 
1390   // Parse URL to extract host, port, and path
1391   // Format: ws://host:port/path or wss://host:port/path
1392   const char *protocol_end = strstr(url, "://");
1393   if (!protocol_end) {
1394     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid WebSocket URL format (missing ://)");
1395     return NULL;
1396   }
1397 
1398   bool use_ssl = (strncmp(url, "wss://", 6) == 0);
1399   const char *host_start = protocol_end + 3;
1400 
1401   // Find port (if specified)
1402   const char *port_start = strchr(host_start, ':');
1403   const char *path_start = strchr(host_start, '/');
1404 
1405   char host[256] = {0};
1406   int port = use_ssl ? 443 : 27226; // Default: wss:// uses 443, ws:// uses 27226 (ascii-chat WebSocket port)
1407   char path[256] = "/";
1408 
1409   if (port_start && (!path_start || port_start < path_start)) {
1410     // Port is specified
1411     size_t host_len = port_start - host_start;
1412     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1413       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1414       return NULL;
1415     }
1416     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1417     host[host_len] = '\0';
1418 
1419     // Extract port
1420     port = atoi(port_start + 1);
1421     if (port <= 0 || port > 65535) {
1422       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid port number");
1423       return NULL;
1424     }
1425   } else {
1426     // No port specified, use default
1427     size_t host_len = path_start ? (size_t)(path_start - host_start) : strlen(host_start);
1428     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1429       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1430       return NULL;
1431     }
1432     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1433     host[host_len] = '\0';
1434   }
1435 
1436   // Extract path
1437   if (path_start) {
1438     SAFE_STRNCPY(path, path_start, sizeof(path) - 1);
1439     path[sizeof(path) - 1] = '\0';
1440   }
1441 
1442   log_info("Connecting to WebSocket: %s (host=%s, port=%d, path=%s, ssl=%d)", url, host, port, path, use_ssl);
1443 
1444   // Allocate transport structure
1445   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1446   if (!transport) {
1447     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1448     return NULL;
1449   }
1450 
1451   // Allocate WebSocket-specific data
1452   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1453       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1454   if (!ws_data) {
1455     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1456     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1457     return NULL;
1458   }
1459 
1460   // Initialize destruction state flag (CALLOC zero-initializes, but explicit for clarity)
1461   atomic_store_bool(&ws_data->is_destroying, false);
1462 
1463   // Create receive queue
1464   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1465   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1466     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1467     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1468     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1469     return NULL;
1470   }
1471 
1472   // Create send queue for client transport to buffer outgoing messages
1473   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1474   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1475     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1476     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1477     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1478     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1479     return NULL;
1480   }
1481 
1482   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1483   char recv_name[64], state_name[64];
1484   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1485   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1486 
1487   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1488     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1489     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1490     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1491     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1492     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1493     return NULL;
1494   }
1495 
1496   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1497     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1498     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1499     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1500     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1501     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1502     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1503     return NULL;
1504   }
1505 
1506   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1507     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1508     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1509     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1510     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1511     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1512     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1513     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1514     return NULL;
1515   }
1516 
1517   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1518     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1519     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1520     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1521     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1522     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1523     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1524     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1525     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1526     return NULL;
1527   }
1528 
1529   // Allocate initial send buffer
1530   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = LWS_PRE + 524288; // Match tx_packet_size (512KB)
1531   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(ws_data->send_buffer_capacity, uint8_t *);
1532   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1533     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1534     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1535     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1536     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1537     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1538     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1539     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1540     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1541     return NULL;
1542   }
1543 
1544   // Create libwebsockets context
1545   // Protocol array must persist for lifetime of context - use static
1546   // NOTE: Define ONLY "acip" protocol for WebSocket connections
1547   // libwebsockets provides built-in "h1" and "http" protocols for WebSocket upgrade handshake.
1548   // When local_protocol_name="h1" and protocol="acip" are specified:
1549   // 1. libwebsockets uses built-in h1 (HTTP/1.1) for initial connection
1550   // 2. HTTP upgrade happens automatically (101 Switching Protocols)
1551   // 3. Connection switches to "acip" protocol using our custom callback
1552   static struct lws_protocols client_protocols[] = {
1553       {
1554           "acip", // ACIP protocol - used after WebSocket upgrade handshake completes
1555           websocket_callback,
1556           0,      // Per-session data - using connect_info.userdata instead
1557           524288, // RX buffer size
1558           0,      // ID
1559           NULL,   // User pointer (set from connect_info.userdata)
1560           524288  // TX packet size
1561       },
1562       {NULL, NULL, 0, 0, 0, NULL, 0} // Terminator
1563   };
1564 
1565   // Disable client compression for now - causes assertion in lws_set_extension_option()
1566   // This is a known issue with libwebsockets permessage-deflate negotiation
1567   // Server-side compression is still enabled, data will be compressed from server to client
1568   // but client->server traffic remains uncompressed (acceptable since client sends less data)
1569   // static const struct lws_extension client_extensions[] = {
1570   //     {"permessage-deflate", lws_extension_callback_pm_deflate, "permessage-deflate; client_max_window_bits=15"},
1571   //     {NULL, NULL, NULL}};
1572 
1573   struct lws_context_creation_info info;
1574   memset(&info, 0, sizeof(info));
1575   info.port = CONTEXT_PORT_NO_LISTEN; // Client mode - no listening
1576   info.protocols = client_protocols;
1577   info.gid = (gid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1578   info.uid = (uid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1579   // Initialize SSL globally for client (required even for clients to use OpenSSL)
1580   // The LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT flag works for both servers and clients
1581   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT;
1582   info.extensions = NULL; // Disable client compression due to lws_set_extension_option() assertion
1583 
1584   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
1585   // Default is 4KB, causing 291KB frames to fragment into 73 × 4KB chunks
1586   // Increase to 512KB to match server and allow larger WebSocket frames without fragmentation
1587   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 512 * 1024; // 512KB per-thread service buffer
1588 
1589   // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
1590   // PING every 30 seconds if idle, close after 35 seconds total with no response
1591   static const lws_retry_bo_t client_keep_alive_policy = {
1592       .secs_since_valid_ping = 30,   // Send PING after 30s idle
1593       .secs_since_valid_hangup = 35, // Hangup if still idle after 35s
1594   };
1595   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &client_keep_alive_policy;
1596 
1597   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
1598   lws_log_init_client();
1599 
1600   ws_data->context = lws_create_context(&info);
1601 
1602   if (!ws_data->context) {
1603     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1604     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1605     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1606     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1607     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1608     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1609     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1610     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1611     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1612     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create libwebsockets context");
1613     return NULL;
1614   }
1615 
1616   // Initialize connection state flags BEFORE calling lws_client_connect_via_info
1617   // The callback might fire immediately, so all fields must be ready
1618   ws_data->is_connected = false;      // Will be set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_ESTABLISHED
1619   ws_data->connection_failed = false; // Set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_CONNECTION_ERROR
1620   ws_data->owns_context = true;       // Client transport owns the context
1621 
1622   // Connect to WebSocket server
1623   log_debug("Initiating WebSocket connection to %s:%d%s", host, port, path);
1624   struct lws_client_connect_info connect_info;
1625   memset(&connect_info, 0, sizeof(connect_info));
1626   connect_info.context = ws_data->context;
1627   connect_info.address = host;
1628   connect_info.port = port;
1629   connect_info.path = path;
1630   connect_info.host = host;
1631   connect_info.origin = host;
1632   // Use h1 (HTTP/1.1) protocol for WebSocket upgrade with acip as the subprotocol
1633   // - local_protocol_name="h1": libwebsockets' built-in HTTP/1.1 handler for WebSocket upgrade
1634   // - protocol="acip": Request this subprotocol from the remote server (Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header)
1635   // libwebsockets internally uses h1 regardless of the protocol name, so we use h1 directly
1636   connect_info.local_protocol_name = "h1";  // libwebsockets built-in HTTP/1.1
1637   connect_info.protocol = "acip";  // Remote subprotocol to request from server
1638   // Use SSL + skip server certificate hostname verification + allow self-signed certs (for development)
1639   connect_info.ssl_connection = use_ssl ? (LCCSCF_USE_SSL | LCCSCF_SKIP_SERVER_CERT_HOSTNAME_CHECK | LCCSCF_ALLOW_SELFSIGNED) : 0;
1640   connect_info.userdata = ws_data;
1641 
1642   log_debug("Calling lws_client_connect_via_info...");
1643   ws_data->wsi = lws_client_connect_via_info(&connect_info);
1644   log_debug("lws_client_connect_via_info returned: %p", (void *)ws_data->wsi);
1645   if (!ws_data->wsi) {
1646     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1647     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1648     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1649     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1650     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1651     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1652     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1653     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1654     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1655     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1656     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to connect to WebSocket server");
1657     return NULL;
1658   }
1659 
1660   // Initialize transport
1661   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1662   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1663   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1664 
1665   // Start service thread immediately - it will drive TLS handshake with frequent polling
1666   // The service thread checks is_destroying and context validity before calling lws_service,
1667   // so it's safe to start it right away without delays.
1668   ws_data->service_running = true;
1669   if (asciichat_thread_create(&ws_data->service_thread, "ws_service", websocket_service_thread, ws_data) != 0) {
1670     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket service thread");
1671     ws_data->service_running = false;
1672     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1673     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1674     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1675     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1676     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1677     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1678     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1679     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1680     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1681     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1682     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to create service thread");
1683     return NULL;
1684   }
1685   log_debug("WebSocket service thread started");
1686 
1687   // CRITICAL FIX: Do NOT block the main thread waiting for connection!
1688   // This prevents stdin/keyboard input from being processed and causes the client to hang.
1689   // The service thread will establish the connection asynchronously.
1690   // The protocol layer (recv) will detect if connection fails and handle it there.
1691   // Return immediately so the main thread can respond to keyboard input.
1692   log_debug("WebSocket transport created, service thread will establish connection asynchronously");
1693 
1694   // Register transport
1695   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1696 
1697   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1698   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1699 
1700   // Return transport immediately - connection will be established by service thread
1701   // If connection fails, recv() will detect is_connected=false and return error
1702   return transport;
1703 }




References asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), lws_log_init_client(),
    mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(), ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and
    WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.


Referenced by server_connection_establish(), and
    websocket_client_connect().






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create
  (const char * name, struct lws * wsi, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. Wraps
    an already-established libwebsockets connection (from server accept). Used
    by websocket_server module to create transports for incoming clients.




Parameters


wsi Established libwebsockets connection (not
  owned by transport)


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Definition at line 1715 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1716                                                                                        {
1717   if (!name) {
1718     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1719     return NULL;
1720   }
1721 
1722   if (!wsi) {
1723     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid wsi parameter");
1724     return NULL;
1725   }
1726 
1727   // Allocate transport structure
1728   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1729   if (!transport) {
1730     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1731     return NULL;
1732   }
1733 
1734   // Allocate transport-specific data
1735   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1736       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1737   if (!ws_data) {
1738     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1739     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1740     return NULL;
1741   }
1742 
1743   // Initialize receive queue
1744   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1745   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1746     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1747     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1748     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1749     return NULL;
1750   }
1751 
1752   // Initialize send queue (for server-side transports)
1753   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1754   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1755     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1756     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1757     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1758     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1759     return NULL;
1760   }
1761 
1762   // Initialize pending-free queue for deferred buffer freeing
1763   // permessage-deflate compression holds buffer references asynchronously,
1764   // so we defer freeing to prevent use-after-free errors
1765   ws_data->pending_free_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(pending_free_item_t), 256);
1766   if (!ws_data->pending_free_queue) {
1767     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1768     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1769     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1770     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1771     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create pending-free queue");
1772     return NULL;
1773   }
1774 
1775   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1776   char recv_name[64], send_name[64], state_name[64], pending_free_name[64];
1777   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1778   snprintf(send_name, sizeof(send_name), "send_%s", name);
1779   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1780   snprintf(pending_free_name, sizeof(pending_free_name), "pending_free_%s", name);
1781 
1782   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1783     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1784     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1785     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1786     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1787     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1788     return NULL;
1789   }
1790 
1791   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1792     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1793     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1794     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1795     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1796     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1797     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1798     return NULL;
1799   }
1800 
1801   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->send_mutex, send_name) != 0) {
1802     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1803     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1804     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1805     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1806     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1807     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1808     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
1809     return NULL;
1810   }
1811 
1812   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1813     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1814     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1815     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1816     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1817     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1818     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1819     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1820     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1821     return NULL;
1822   }
1823 
1824   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1825     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1826     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1827     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1828     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1829     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1830     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1831     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1832     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1833     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1834     return NULL;
1835   }
1836 
1837   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->pending_free_mutex, pending_free_name) != 0) {
1838     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1839     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1840     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1841     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1842     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1843     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1844     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1845     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->pending_free_queue);
1846     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1847     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1848     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize pending-free mutex");
1849     return NULL;
1850   }
1851 
1852   // Allocate send buffer with LWS_PRE padding
1853   size_t initial_capacity = 4096 + LWS_PRE;
1854   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(initial_capacity, uint8_t *);
1855   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1856     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1857     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1858     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1859     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1860     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1861     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1862     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1863     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1864     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1865     return NULL;
1866   }
1867   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = initial_capacity;
1868 
1869   // Store connection info (server-side: no context ownership, connection already established)
1870   ws_data->wsi = wsi;
1871   ws_data->context = lws_get_context(wsi); // Get context from wsi (not owned)
1872   ws_data->owns_context = false;           // Server owns context, not transport
1873   ws_data->is_connected = true;            // Already connected (server-side)
1874   log_info("[WEBSOCKET_TRANSPORT_CREATE] ★★★ SERVER TRANSPORT CREATED: is_connected=true, wsi=%p, ws_data=%p",
1875            (void *)wsi, (void *)ws_data);
1876   log_debug("Server transport created: is_connected=true, wsi=%p", (void *)wsi);
1877 
1878   // Initialize transport
1879   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1880   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1881   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1882 
1883   log_info("Created WebSocket server transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
1884 
1885   // Register transport
1886   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1887 
1888   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1889   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1890 
1891   return transport;
1892 }






References mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(),
    ringbuffer_create(), ringbuffer_destroy(),
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.0
    ascii-chat
